raw_consumer_statement: I prepaid for hotel rooms on XX/XX/2024 for a hotel XXXX, XXXX using my Barclays ( XXXX XXXX ) credit card. The booking was for three ( 3 ) rooms with two queen beds in each room that sleeps 4 persons per room. Upon arrival at the hotel once we landed in XXXX on XX/XX/2024 I was advised by the front desk manager that there's no guarantee for my rooms and those rooms were no longer available and basically was not willing to assist in resolving the situation. The manager instructed me to cancel and get a refund I needed to send an email, which I did the next day. Credit card company dispute was filed the same night XX/XX/2024 and they closed my dispute in XXXX stating per Merchant I was a no show even though I sent them a picture of me in the hotel lobby with said manager in the background, documents of having to get another hotel for the SAME dates and correspondence between me and hotel about the cancellation and refund. I filed a second dispute after the original dispute was closed and submitted all my evidence showing that I was not a no show as claimed by merchant. Credit card company once again closed the dispute now claiming past time to file which makes no sense when I was instructed by their employee to then submit my proof to dispute what the merchant was saying. I have since contacted this credit card company several times requesting assistance since I am now being held liable for a service that was never rendered even though it was prepaid!
